title: Dispatch Overview
lede: Dispatch is iMash's field-service headquarters — every job, technician, text message, and dollar of your field business on one screen. This guide tours the whole Dispatch section: the Statistics home view, every tab in the strip, and the Settings hub that powers your messaging and auto-dispatch automations.

steps.title: Open the Dispatch section
steps.content:
If you run technicians in the field — plumbers, garage-door techs, locksmiths, movers, anyone — Dispatch is where iMash runs that business for you: jobs come in, technicians get texted automatically, customers get updates, and the money is tracked job by job.

  1. In the top navigation bar, click Dispatch.
  2. You land on the Statistics view — the section's default home — with a second row of colorful chips right below the navigation bar. That chip strip is how you move between every Dispatch view.
💡 Every view in this section is deep-linkable — the address bar updates as you switch chips, so you can bookmark or share any tab (for example, the Kanban board) directly.

images.alt: The Dispatch section open on the Statistics view
images.caption: Dispatch home: the chip strip under the navigation bar, date filters, KPI cards, and charts.

images.alt: The Dispatch section open on the Statistics view
images.caption: Dispatch home: the chip strip under the navigation bar, date filters, KPI cards, and charts.

steps.title: Read your business at a glance in Statistics
steps.content:
The Statistics view answers the owner's question — "how are we doing?" — with real revenue math, not vanity numbers. Start with the toolbar:

  1. Pick a date preset — Today, Yesterday, This Week, Last Week, Last 7 days, This Month, Last Month, Last 30 days, or Custom — or set exact dates in the range picker next to it.
  2. Check the Filter By chip. It defaults to Completion Date, so revenue is counted in the period the work was finished — click the arrow to switch to other date fields (like the date the job was added) if you prefer.
  3. Click the refresh icon any time to recompute.

The KPI cards then update instantly:

  • Jobs — how many jobs fall in the period.
  • Total — the actual amount charged on paid invoices (including tax, net of refunds). Tips are excluded — they get their own card.
  • Tips — customer tips collected on paid invoices. Tips go 100% to the technician, so they're kept separate from company revenue.
  • Company's Cut — your share after technician commissions.
  • Cancelled and Completed — job counts with their percentage of the total, so you can watch your close rate.
  • Calls — calls linked to dispatch activity in the period.

images.alt: The Statistics date toolbar and KPI cards
images.caption: Date presets, the Completion Date filter, and the KPI cards: Jobs, Total, Tips, Company's Cut, Cancelled, Completed, and Calls.

images.alt: The Statistics date toolbar and KPI cards
images.caption: Date presets, the Completion Date filter, and the KPI cards: Jobs, Total, Tips, Company's Cut, Cancelled, Completed, and Calls.

steps.title: Dig deeper with the Statistics charts
steps.content:
Scroll down for the visual breakdowns — each chart respects the same date range you picked above:

  • Top Team Members — your best performers ranked by completed work.
  • Top Performance By — a leaderboard you can pivot between Job Source and Job Type, so you see which lead sources or service categories actually deliver.
  • Tickets by Status — a donut of every job by its current status (scheduled, dispatched, completed, cancelled…).
  • Jobs by Area — where your work is happening, city by city.
  • Job Sources by Sales Amount and Job Sources by Done Jobs — which sources bring the revenue vs. which bring the volume.
  • Earnings Breakdown (Last 12 Months) — a month-by-month bar view of Total, Company's Cut, and Tech's Cut, so trends jump out.
💡 Hover any chart segment for exact numbers, and use the legends to identify each slice.

images.alt: The Statistics charts: leaderboards, status donut, and area breakdown
images.caption: The chart grid: Top Team Members, Top Performance By, Tickets by Status, Jobs by Area, and job-source breakdowns.

images.alt: The Statistics charts: leaderboards, status donut, and area breakdown
images.caption: The chart grid: Top Team Members, Top Performance By, Tickets by Status, Jobs by Area, and job-source breakdowns.

steps.title: Tour the Dispatch tab strip
steps.content:
The chip strip under the navigation bar is your map of the whole section. Scroll it sideways with the arrow at its edge — here's what each chip does:

  • Statistics — the KPI and chart home view you just explored.
  • Dispatch Dashboard — the job command center: every job in a live table with status tabs, filters, and the Create Job button (full walkthrough in the Jobs guide).
  • Kanban View — the same jobs as a drag-and-drop board, one column per status.
  • Map — technicians and jobs plotted live on a map.
  • Messages — the dispatch texting inbox: every SMS conversation with customers and technicians in one place.
  • Team Members — your technician roster: availability, priority order, commission plans, and devices (see the Technicians & Team Members guide).
  • Managers — give a lead-source partner a read-only reporting portal for the jobs they send you.
  • Job Sources, Job Types, Payment Types — the catalogs that classify every job: where it came from, what kind of work it is, and how it was paid (see the Job Sources & Types guide).
  • Finance Reports and Team Reports — deep money and per-technician reporting with exports (see the Dispatch Reports guide).
  • Waiting ACK — a live board of dispatches waiting for a technician to reply ACK (accept) — the heartbeat of auto-dispatch.
  • Simulator — a safe sandbox that runs your entire auto-dispatch flow with fake jobs and technicians, so you can test rules without texting anyone real.
  • Trace — a step-by-step event log of every dispatch: every text sent, every candidate scored, every state change.
  • Simulate SMS — an action chip that opens a composer to inject a test inbound text, exactly as if a customer had texted your number.
  • Settings — an action chip that opens the Dispatch Settings hub (next-to-last step below).

images.alt: The Dispatch chip strip with all its tabs
images.caption: The tab strip: Statistics, Dispatch Dashboard, Kanban View, Map, Messages, Team Members, Managers, and more — scroll for the rest.

images.alt: The Dispatch chip strip with all its tabs
images.caption: The tab strip: Statistics, Dispatch Dashboard, Kanban View, Map, Messages, Team Members, Managers, and more — scroll for the rest.

steps.title: Visit the Dispatch Dashboard — your job command center
steps.content:
Click Dispatch Dashboard in the strip. This is where dispatchers live all day:

  • Date controls — a preset picker (including Recently Updated), a custom range, and an Added Date / Scheduled Start / Scheduled End switch that decides which date the range filters by.
  • Counter tiles — live counts like Jobs, Draft, In Progress, Completed, Urgent, and Available Techs. Which tiles show is configurable in Dispatch Settings.
  • Status tabs — All Jobs, Draft, In Progress, Completed, Scheduled, Dispatched, On Hold, Cancelled, Unassigned, Urgent, and more — each with its live count, filtering the table to that slice.
  • Filters — opens a drawer to narrow by technician, status, job type, job source, or owner; the badge on the button counts active filters.
  • Create Job — the blue button in the top-right starts a new job.

Every row links into the full job detail — status, finance, documents, messages, and dispatch controls. The complete walkthrough lives in the Jobs guide.

images.alt: The Dispatch Dashboard with counter tiles, status tabs, and the jobs table
images.caption: The Dispatch Dashboard: date controls, counter tiles, status tabs, Filters, and Create Job.

images.alt: The Dispatch Dashboard with counter tiles, status tabs, and the jobs table
images.caption: The Dispatch Dashboard: date controls, counter tiles, status tabs, Filters, and Create Job.

steps.title: Drag jobs across the Kanban board
steps.content:
Prefer a visual pipeline? Click Kanban View:

  1. Each column is a job status — Draft, Scheduled, Dispatched, In Progress, On Hold, Completed — with a live count in its header.
  2. Each card shows the job's title, address, and how long ago it was created (or when it's scheduled).
  3. Drag a card from one column to another to change the job's status — no forms, no clicks into the record.
  4. The same date controls from the Dispatch Dashboard sit on top, so the board can show today, this week, or any range you need.
⚠️ Dragging a card really changes the job's status — and any status-change automations you've enabled (like customer SMS updates) will fire. Move cards with intent!

images.alt: The Kanban board with status columns and job cards
images.caption: Kanban View: one column per status — drag a card to change a job's status instantly.

images.alt: The Kanban board with status columns and job cards
images.caption: Kanban View: one column per status — drag a card to change a job's status instantly.

steps.title: See your fleet on the Map
steps.content:
Click Map to plot your whole operation geographically — technicians with GPS tracking enabled appear as live pins, and every job with a coordinates-resolved address gets a status-colored marker:

  • The counters in the top-left show how many technicians and jobs are currently plotted.
  • Use the Technician picker to focus on one tech's pin and jobs.
  • Toggle Added / Scheduled and pick a day preset (like Today) to control which jobs are drawn.
  • The status multi-select (e.g. Draft +7) filters markers by job status; Reset clears everything back to defaults.
  • The legend underneath decodes the colors — technicians as Available, Busy, or Offline/Stale, and jobs by their status color.
  • Click any technician or job marker to open its details right from the map.
💡 Seeing "No locations to display"? Technicians need GPS tracking enabled (they share location from the technician mobile app), and jobs need real street addresses so iMash can resolve coordinates.

images.alt: The Map view with its filter toolbar and legend
images.caption: The Map view: technician and job counters, filters, day presets, and the color legend.

images.alt: The Map view with its filter toolbar and legend
images.caption: The Map view: technician and job counters, filters, day presets, and the color legend.

steps.title: Meet your roster in Team Members
steps.content:
Click Team Members for the technician roster that powers dispatching:

  • Four tiles summarize the team: Total Team Members, Active Team Members, Available Now, and On Assignment.
  • The table lists every member with their Status (active/inactive), Tag, dispatch Priority (reorder with the arrows or by dragging the handle), Availability, Performance (jobs completed), Commission plan, and connected Devices.
  • Click + Add Team Member in the top-right to create a new technician.

Priority order matters: sequential auto-dispatch texts technicians one at a time in this order until someone accepts. The full setup — commissions, working hours, service areas, devices — is covered in the Technicians & Team Members guide.

images.alt: The Team Members roster with summary tiles and the technician table
images.caption: Team Members: summary tiles, the roster with priority, availability, performance, and commissions, and Add Team Member.

images.alt: The Team Members roster with summary tiles and the technician table
images.caption: Team Members: summary tiles, the roster with priority, availability, performance, and commissions, and Add Team Member.

steps.title: Open Dispatch Settings — the automation hub
steps.content:
The Settings chip at the end of the strip opens the Dispatch Settings modal — the single place where all dispatch messaging and automation is configured:

  1. Click Settings in the tab strip.
  2. At the top, set Send SMS From — the phone number all outbound dispatch texts are sent from. (No numbers listed? Assign a messaging profile to one of your numbers under Phone Numbers first.)
  3. Work through the sections in the left rail — each one is an independent automation with its own On/Off toggle:
    • JOB SMS — New Job and Job Update: text a recipient list whenever a job is created or changed, with match rules deciding exactly when it fires.
    • INBOUND — SMS → Ticket (turn inbound customer texts into jobs automatically), Status-Change SMS (notify customers as their job moves through statuses), Stale-Status (nudge when a job sits too long), and SMS Calendar Reminder (appointment reminders).
    • AUTO DISPATCH — Reply Handling, OTW Confirmation, Outbound Dispatch, Broadcast / Race, and Calendar Sync: the engine that texts jobs to technicians and processes their ACK/PASS replies.
  4. Click Save when you change anything, or Close to exit.
💡 Auto-dispatch is the crown jewel — iMash texting the right technician the moment a job lands, escalating until someone accepts. Set it up end to end with the Auto Dispatch guide below.

images.alt: The Dispatch Settings modal with its section rail and toggles
images.caption: Dispatch Settings: Send SMS From on top, then Job SMS, Inbound, and Auto Dispatch sections — each with its own On/Off toggle.

images.alt: The Dispatch Settings modal with its section rail and toggles
images.caption: Dispatch Settings: Send SMS From on top, then Job SMS, Inbound, and Auto Dispatch sections — each with its own On/Off toggle.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Questions about setting up your dispatch operation? Email us at support@imash.io and our team will help you get every job, technician, and automation dialed in.
